# UC-22.20.15 · CMMC Level 3 enhanced practice — threat scenario 5

## Description

Demonstrates contractor cybersecurity hygiene for "CMMC Level 3 enhanced practice — threat scenario 5" using CUI-scoped telemetry mapped to CMMC 2.0 Level 3 expectations.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for CMMC compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes vuln and sourcetypes tenable:was. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Confirm field extractions and CIM tags for the sourcetypes in scope; (2) Load or maintain the referenced lookups/KVStore collections with owner attestation dates; (3) Schedule the search at an interval aligned to the control’s materiality; (4) Route positive findings to the compliance ticketing queue with required evidence fields; (5) Retain scheduled search exports per records management and legal hold procedures. Indexes required: `index=vuln`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for CrowdStrike FDR (5082), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (4055), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621)..
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `WinEventLog:Security`, CrowdStrike FDR, Defender for Office 365, NetFlow, encryption posture exports.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Confirm field extractions and CIM tags for the sourcetypes in scope; (2) Load or maintain the referenced lookups/KVStore collections with owner attestation dates; (3) Schedule the search at an interval aligned to the control’s materiality; (4) Route positive findings to the compliance ticketing queue with required evidence fields; (5) Retain scheduled search exports per records management and legal hold procedures.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=vuln sourcetype=tenable:was earliest=-14d
| eval uc_tag="22.20.15"
| where scan_name="CUI-Web" AND risk="High"
| stats dc(url) as urls by plugin_id
```

Understanding this SPL

**CMMC Level 3 enhanced practice — threat scenario 5 (CMMC 2.0 Level 3)** — Demonstrates contractor cybersecurity hygiene for "CMMC Level 3 enhanced practice — threat scenario 5" using CUI-scoped telemetry mapped to CMMC 2.0 Level 3 expectations.

Documented **Data sources**: `WinEventLog:Security`, CrowdStrike FDR, Defender for Office 365, NetFlow, encryption posture exports. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for CrowdStrike FDR (5082), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (4055), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: vuln; **sourcetype**: tenable:was. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=vuln, sourcetype=tenable:was, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **uc_tag** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where scan_name="CUI-Web" AND risk="High"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by plugin_id** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes by Processes.process_name, Processes.user, Processes.dest | sort - count
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**CMMC Level 3 enhanced practice — threat scenario 5 (CMMC 2.0 Level 3)** — Demonstrates contractor cybersecurity hygiene for "CMMC Level 3 enhanced practice — threat scenario 5" using CUI-scoped telemetry mapped to CMMC 2.0 Level 3 expectations.

Documented **Data sources**: `WinEventLog:Security`, CrowdStrike FDR, Defender for Office 365, NetFlow, encryption posture exports. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for CrowdStrike FDR (5082), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (4055), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Endpoint.Processes` — enable acceleration for that model.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with the GRC system, audit register, or framework crosswalk. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Stacked bar, Table, Time chart, Single value

## SPL

```spl
index=vuln sourcetype=tenable:was earliest=-14d
| eval uc_tag="22.20.15"
| where scan_name="CUI-Web" AND risk="High"
| stats dc(url) as urls by plugin_id
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Vulnerabilities.Vulnerabilities by Vulnerabilities.severity, Vulnerabilities.dest, Vulnerabilities.signature | sort - count
```

## Visualization

Stacked bar, Table, Time chart, Single value. Key fields: `plugin_id`, `uc_tag`, `urls`.
